semantic.role: Public facade module that re-exports prompt-context policy constants and diagnostic functions, providing a stable import boundary for consumers and hiding internal submodule structure.
responsibilities: Re-export STRICT_VALIDATE_BUDGET_PROFILES, VALIDATE_POLICIES, PROMPT_BUDGET_PROFILES constants from ./prompt-policy.js.
Re-export parseRelevanceDiagnosticsMode, parseValidatePolicy, defaultValidatePolicyForTask, resolveValidatePolicy from internal prompt-context modules.
Enforce that all external consumers import prompt-context functionality exclusively through this file.
outOfScope: Implementation details of the re-exported functions and constants.
Direct imports from src/infra/prompt-context/* submodules by other consumers.
invariants: This file is the sole public entry point for prompt-context policy and diagnostics functionality.
External modules must not import directly from src/infra/prompt-context/*; they must use this facade.
The facade itself contains no implementation logic – it only re-exports symbols from internal modules.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Decouple prompt-context consumers from internal module structure by providing a stable re-export facade.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: chore: apply release gate fixes and sync accumulated source changes – no semantic change to this file's contract.

publicSurface.description: Constant that defines strict budget profile validation mode. Used by prompt policy evaluation.
Constant that enables validation policies for prompt budgets. Used by prompt context building.
Constant that holds the set of available prompt budget profiles. Used to limit source prompt size.
Constructs source prompt artifacts from repository index data. Called during prompt assembly.
Calculates budget allocations for source prompts based on profiles. Called by prompt builder.
Parses a string into a relevance diagnostics mode enum. Used by context relevance engine.
Parses a policy string into a validate policy value. Used for policy resolution.
Returns the default validate policy for a given task type. Used when no explicit policy is set.
Resolves the effective validate policy from task configuration and defaults. Called by the validation pipeline.
